name: DividedDifferenceInterpolator
javadoc:
/** * Implements the * Divided Difference Algorithm for interpolation of real univariate * functions. For reference, see Introduction to Numerical Analysis, * ISBN 038795452X, chapter 2. *

* The actual code of Neville's evaluation is in PolynomialFunctionLagrangeForm, * this class provides an easy-to-use interface to it.

* * @since 1.2 */

members.name: interpolate
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Compute an interpolating function for the dataset.
 *
 * @param x Interpolating points array.
 * @param y Interpolating values array.
 * @return a function which interpolates the dataset.
 * @throws DimensionMismatchException if the array lengths are different.
 * @throws NumberIsTooSmallException if the number of points is less than 2.
 * @throws NonMonotonicSequenceException if {@code x} is not sorted in
 * strictly increasing order.
 */

tags.description: a function which interpolates the dataset.

tags.name: DimensionMismatchException
tags.content: if the array lengths are different.
tags.description: if the array lengths are different.

tags.name: NumberIsTooSmallException
tags.content: if the number of points is less than 2.
tags.description: the number of points is less than 2.

tags.name: NonMonotonicSequenceException
tags.content: if {@code x} is not sorted in strictly increasing order.

members.name: computeDividedDifference
members.javadoc:
/** * Return a copy of the divided difference array. *

* The divided difference array is defined recursively by 

 * f[x0] = f(x0)
 * f[x0,x1,...,xk] = (f[x1,...,xk] - f[x0,...,x[k-1]]) / (xk - x0)
 *
*

* The computational complexity is \(O(n^2)\) where \(n\) is the common * length of {@code x} and {@code y}.

* * @param x Interpolating points array. * @param y Interpolating values array. * @return a fresh copy of the divided difference array. * @throws DimensionMismatchException if the array lengths are different. * @throws NumberIsTooSmallException if the number of points is less than 2. * @throws NonMonotonicSequenceException * if {@code x} is not sorted in strictly increasing order. */

tags.content: fresh copy of the divided difference array.
tags.description: a fresh copy of the divided difference array.

tags.content: if the array lengths are different.
